skill.name: image-to-code
skill.description: Guides Codex, Claude, and Claude Code through image-first website design, visual analysis, and faithful frontend implementation.
skill.summary: Create visual website references before coding and translate them into polished frontend layouts.
skill.author: leonxlnx

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, sensitive file, weak crypto, and reconnaissance indicators, but review found prose-only design instructions. The backticks are Markdown formatting, and the other alerts are keyword collisions in words such as design, system, seamless, and spacing guidance. No executable scripts, network calls, credential access, or prompt-injection attempts were found in the reviewed skill file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backtick Configuration Values
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Lines flagged as Ruby or shell backtick execution are Markdown-formatted numeric design settings, not executable commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain inline Markdown examples for values from 1 to 10. No shell command, interpreter invocation, or user-controlled command string is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Design Prose Misread as Weak Crypto
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Weak-cryptography alerts map to ordinary design and implementation prose, not cryptographic functions or algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill is a Markdown instruction document about website design. The cited lines do not define MD5, SHA1, DES, cipher modes, hashes, or security-sensitive code.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Website System Heading Misread as Windows SAM
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The sensitive-file alert occurs on a heading about a website image system, not the Windows SAM database.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact context is a Markdown section title for visual design rules. There is no filesystem path, registry reference, credential database access, or Windows-specific instruction.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Design-System Language Misread as System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. System reconnaissance alerts come from design-system terminology and layout guidance, not host inspection commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines discuss visual systems, spacing, labels, and content style. No uname, whoami, hostname, environment enumeration, or filesystem discovery behavior is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Copy and Spacing Terms Misread as Network Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Network reconnaissance alerts come from harmless words in copy guidance and spacing rules.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 933 is a discouraged marketing word, and line 1008 concerns negative space in layout. No port scanning, DNS lookup, socket use, or network target enumeration appears.

content.user_title: Generate Image-Led Websites From Visual References
content.value_statement: Visual website builds often drift from the intended design. This skill forces an image-first workflow, detailed visual analysis, and faithful frontend implementation.
actual_capabilities: Guides Codex to generate design images before coding visual websites.
Encourages one large reference image per requested section.
Requires detailed analysis of typography, spacing, colors, buttons, and media.
Discourages nested cards, crowded sections, fake labels, and generic layouts.
Defines example workflows for hero sections and multi-section landing pages.
limitations: It depends on available image generation and visual inspection tools.
It does not provide executable scripts or reusable component code.
It may increase generation time for large multi-section websites.
It cannot guarantee pixel-perfect output without manual review.

use_cases.title: Design a Premium Landing Page
use_cases.description: Create section-level visual references, analyze them, and implement a polished marketing page.

use_cases.title: Improve Frontend Visual Fidelity
use_cases.description: Use generated images as the visual source of truth before writing responsive frontend code.

use_cases.title: Explore Art-Directed Website Concepts
use_cases.description: Generate distinct section references that avoid generic card layouts and crowded hero screens.

prompt_templates.title: Create One Hero Section
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for a single visual section.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design one premium hero section for a new product. Generate the visual reference first, analyze it, then implement it.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Four-Section Site
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt for a compact landing page.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a four-section landing page. Generate one clear image per section, extract typography and spacing, then build the site from those references.

prompt_templates.title: Refresh an Existing Landing Page
prompt_templates.prompt: Redesign this landing page with stronger visual direction. Generate fresh section references, avoid nested card layouts, and preserve readable responsive structure.

prompt_templates.title: Produce a Visual-to-Code Build Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for careful implementation planning.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate section images for an eight-section website. Analyze colors, type, spacing, buttons, and media before implementing the complete frontend.

output_examples.input: A founder asks for a premium hero section for an AI startup.
output_examples.output: The assistant generates a hero reference image, extracts layout details, then builds a calm responsive hero with matching type and media.

output_examples.input: A team asks for an eight-section product landing page.
output_examples.output: The assistant creates separate section references, checks consistency, and implements a page that avoids tiny text and repeated generic blocks.

output_examples.input: A designer wants a more editorial portfolio homepage.
output_examples.output: The assistant explores stronger typography and image rhythm, then translates the selected visual direction into frontend structure.

best_practices: Use clear product, audience, and section goals before asking for image generation.
Review each generated section image before implementation starts.
Regenerate unclear sections instead of guessing layout details.
anti_patterns: Do not skip visual references for design-sensitive website work.
Do not compress many sections into one unreadable image board.
Do not accept generic nested cards when the reference uses stronger structure.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create image-led website concepts and translate them into frontend implementations.

faq.question: Does it write code directly?
faq.answer: It guides the assistant to generate and analyze images before writing frontend code.

faq.question: Which tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is this useful for non-visual tasks?
faq.answer: It is best for visual website tasks where design fidelity matters.

faq.question: Can it handle multi-section websites?
faq.answer: Yes. It recommends separate readable images for each major section.

faq.question: Does it include executable helper scripts?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed package contains a Markdown instruction file only.
